原文: Music has long been considered to be an enjoyable pastime① for many people. F. It is also highly popular due to the individualized effects on stress and anxiety②. The mental health benefits③ from music can't be argued. Music could also be helping you with many other health problems behind the scenes④.
翻译: 长久以来，音乐被许多人视为一种令人愉悦的消遣。F. 由于它对压力和焦虑的个性化影响，音乐也非常受欢迎。音乐对心理健康的好处是毋庸置疑的。音乐也可能在幕后帮助你应对许多其他健康问题。
必背词汇:
① pastime /ˈpɑːstaɪm/ n. 消遣，娱乐
Ⓡ past /pɑːst/ adj. 过去的 n. 过去
Ⓡ time /taɪm/ n. 时间
Ⓡ entertainment /ˌentəˈteɪnmənt/ n. 娱乐
② anxiety /æŋˈzaɪəti/ n. 焦虑，忧虑
Ⓡ anxious /ˈæŋkʃəs/ adj. 焦虑的
Ⓡ anxiety disorder 焦虑症
③ benefit /ˈbenɪfɪt/ n. 好处，益处 v. 受益
Ⓡ beneficial /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃl/ adj. 有益的
Ⓡ beneficiary /ˌbenɪˈfɪʃəri/ n. 受益人
④ scene /siːn/ n. 场景；幕后
Ⓡ behind the scenes 在幕后，秘密地
Ⓡ scenery /ˈsiːnəri/ n. 风景

原文: B. Some experts say that music can be harmful⑤ if it is too loud. However, for the same reason, music can be very beneficial if one is in pain⑥. By distracting (分心) the mind from the pain, music, people say, can lower stress and anxiety levels. This, of course, can lead to less pain.
翻译: B. 一些专家说，如果音乐太大声，它可能是有害的。然而，出于同样的原因，如果一个人处于疼痛中，音乐可能非常有益。人们说，通过分散对疼痛的注意力，音乐可以降低压力和焦虑水平。这当然可以导致疼痛减轻。
必背词汇:
⑤ harmful /ˈhɑːmfl/ adj. 有害的
Ⓡ harm /hɑːm/ n. & v. 伤害
Ⓡ harmless /ˈhɑːmləs/ adj. 无害的
⑥ pain /peɪn/ n. 疼痛；痛苦
Ⓡ painful /ˈpeɪnfl/ adj. 疼痛的
Ⓡ painkiller /ˈpeɪnkɪlə(r)/ n. 止痛药

原文: Many people enjoy relaxing⑦ music in the evening prior to going to bed. D. They say it can play a big role in calming⑧ the brain enough to sleep. While the validity⑨ of the idea is still being assessed⑩, the lowered stress can even be tied back to blood pressure⑪. Similarly, according to researchers, listening to just 30 minutes of soft music every day may help with healthy blood sugar⑫ levels, through the lowering of stress and anxiety.
翻译: 许多人喜欢在晚上睡觉前听放松的音乐。D. 他们说，音乐可以在使大脑平静到足以入睡方面发挥重要作用。虽然这个想法的有效性仍在评估中，但降低的压力甚至可能与血压有关。同样，根据研究人员的说法，每天只听30分钟轻柔的音乐，通过降低压力和焦虑，可能有助于维持健康的血糖水平。
必背词汇:
⑦ relax /rɪˈlæks/ v. 放松
Ⓡ relaxing /rɪˈlæksɪŋ/ adj. 令人放松的
Ⓡ relaxation /ˌriːlækˈseɪʃn/ n. 放松
⑧ calm /kɑːm/ v. 使平静 adj. 平静的
Ⓡ calm down 平静下来
Ⓡ calmness /ˈkɑːmnəs/ n. 平静
⑨ validity /vəˈlɪdəti/ n. 有效性；正确性
Ⓡ valid /ˈvælɪd/ adj. 有效的；有根据的
Ⓡ invalid /ɪnˈvælɪd/ adj. 无效的
⑩ assess /əˈses/ v. 评估；评定
Ⓡ assessment /əˈsesmənt/ n. 评估
Ⓡ assessor /əˈsesə(r)/ n. 评估员
⑪ pressure /ˈpreʃə(r)/ n. 压力
Ⓡ press /pres/ v. 按压 n. 新闻界
Ⓡ blood pressure 血压
⑫ sugar /ˈʃʊɡə(r)/ n. 糖
Ⓡ blood sugar 血糖
Ⓡ sugary /ˈʃʊɡəri/ adj. 含糖的

原文: When it comes to heart health, there is speculation (推测) that it's not the style of music, but rather the tempo⑬ that makes it so good for your heart health. In one European study, participants⑭ listened to music as the researchers monitored⑮ their heart rates⑯ and blood pressure. G. Interestingly, the more cheerful⑰ the music was, the faster their heart rates were. On the other hand, when the music slowed, the participants' stress and anxiety levels became lower and the effects on heart rates appeared to follow suit⑱.
翻译: 当谈到心脏健康时，有推测认为，不是音乐的风格，而是节奏使其对心脏健康如此有益。在一项欧洲研究中，参与者听音乐，同时研究人员监测他们的心率和血压。G. 有趣的是，音乐越欢快，他们的心率就越快。另一方面，当音乐变慢时，参与者的压力和焦虑水平降低，对心率的影响似乎也随之变化。
必背词汇:
⑬ tempo /ˈtempəʊ/ n. 节奏；速度
Ⓡ temporary /ˈtemprəri/ adj. 临时的
Ⓡ temperature /ˈtemprətʃə(r)/ n. 温度
⑭ participant /pɑːˈtɪsɪpənt/ n. 参与者
Ⓡ participate /pɑːˈtɪsɪpeɪt/ v. 参与
Ⓡ participation /pɑːˌtɪsɪˈpeɪʃn/ n. 参与
⑮ monitor /ˈmɒnɪtə(r)/ v. 监测；监视 n. 显示器
Ⓡ monitor screen 监视器屏幕
Ⓡ monitoring /ˈmɒnɪtərɪŋ/ n. 监测
⑯ rate /reɪt/ n. 比率；速度
Ⓡ heart rate 心率
Ⓡ exchange rate 汇率
⑰ cheerful /ˈtʃɪəfl/ adj. 欢快的；高兴的
Ⓡ cheer /tʃɪə(r)/ v. 欢呼 n. 欢呼声
Ⓡ cheer up 振作起来
⑱ suit /suːt/ v. 适合 n. 套装
Ⓡ follow suit 跟着做，效仿
Ⓡ suitable /ˈsuːtəbl/ adj. 合适的

原文: E. The implications⑲ of music on overall well-being⑳ are really impressive. But there is a whole range of other health issues that turning up the radio could be beneficial for, which is what makes music so valuable.
翻译: E. 音乐对整体健康的影响确实令人印象深刻。但还有一系列其他健康问题，调高收音机音量可能对其有益，这正是音乐如此有价值的原因。
必背词汇:
⑲ implication /ˌɪmplɪˈkeɪʃn/ n. 影响；含义
Ⓡ imply /ɪmˈplaɪ/ v. 暗示
Ⓡ implicit /ɪmˈplɪsɪt/ adj. 含蓄的
⑳ well-being /ˌwel ˈbiːɪŋ/ n. 健康；幸福
Ⓡ welfare /ˈwelfeə(r)/ n. 福利
Ⓡ wellbeing index 幸福指数
